Exact_Claim: Ivana was an accomplished competitive skier and later worked as a ski instructor.
Supporting_Evidence: Library and Archives Canada 1975 catalog describes her as skiing instructor and model; consistent later accounts.
New_Expansion: Provides contemporaneous Canadian support independent of later Trump branding.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Exact competitive level and results remain unverified.
Additional_Record_Needed: Club records; school records; Czechoslovak ski federation files; race results.

Exact_Claim: Ivana was a member of a Czechoslovak junior national ski team.
Supporting_Evidence: Repeated in biographies and obituaries.
New_Expansion: Separates junior-team claim from Olympic claims rather than treating them as interchangeable.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No contemporary roster located in this research pass.
Additional_Record_Needed: Junior federation rosters; Zlin and Prague club files; sports-ministry records.

Exact_Claim: Ivana competed as a Czechoslovak alpine skier at the 1972 Sapporo Olympics.
Supporting_Evidence: None located.
New_Expansion: Official Czech Olympic and FIS records show no Czechoslovak women's alpine contingent and no Ivana.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: An alternate would not necessarily appear in results, but a competitor claim is unsupported.
Additional_Record_Needed: Complete delegation and alternate roster; Czech Olympic Committee correspondence.

Exact_Claim: Ivana was an alternate for the 1972 Czechoslovak Olympic ski team.
Supporting_Evidence: Repeated by Ivana and Donald Trump in public biographies.
New_Expansion: Official roster work makes the alternate claim a distinct question rather than accepted fact.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No official alternate roster found; Czech Olympic official reportedly found no record.
Additional_Record_Needed: Czech Olympic Committee archival inquiry; sports ministry files.

Exact_Claim: Ivana claimed membership in a Canadian Olympic ski team.
Supporting_Evidence: Washington Post/Reuters report of federal trademark complaint.
New_Expansion: Reveals a conflicting Canadian Olympic formulation in a legal-commercial context.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Original complaint not yet obtained; the claim itself is not substantiated.
Additional_Record_Needed: Federal complaint and exhibits in Ivana Trump v. Pavion Ltd.; docket and pleadings.

Exact_Claim: Czechoslovak citizens required state-issued travel documents and exit authorization to cross the border.
Supporting_Evidence: Act 63/1965 and Decree 44/1970.
New_Expansion: Grounds the mobility question in actual law rather than general Cold War description.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Exact implementation in Ivana's case is unknown.
Additional_Record_Needed: Passport file; exit endorsement; district and Interior Ministry records.

Exact_Claim: Ivana married Alfred Winklmayr to obtain Austrian status and leave Czechoslovakia.
Evidence_Classification: Interested-party statement corroborated in FBI biographical record
Supporting_Evidence: Ivana memoir account; FBI immigration review reporting.
New_Expansion: Retains original conclusion and connects it to later FBI marriage-network lead.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Underlying marriage and nationality files are not public.
Additional_Record_Needed: Marriage certificate; Austrian citizenship and passport file; divorce record.

Exact_Claim: The StB or another intelligence-linked intermediary arranged the Winklmayr marriage.
Supporting_Evidence: Marriage served an exit purpose; FBI later examined a Czech-intelligence-linked fictitious-marriage facilitator.
New_Expansion: The FBI lead makes marriage facilitation a concrete investigative avenue.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No public record connects the redacted facilitator to Winklmayr or Ivana's marriage.
Additional_Record_Needed: Unredacted FBI files; Austrian and Czech records; identity of redacted subject.

Exact_Claim: Ivana obtained Austrian citizenship or passport status shortly after marrying Winklmayr.
Supporting_Evidence: FBI immigration review and repeated biographies.
New_Expansion: Places exact legal mechanism on the records plan.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Citizenship and passport are distinct statuses; date and basis need proof.
Additional_Record_Needed: Austrian nationality and passport files.

Exact_Claim: Ivana worked in Canada as a ski instructor and model.
Supporting_Evidence: Library and Archives Canada item 3232626.
New_Expansion: Replaces weak later citations with a dated archival record.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Catalog metadata may derive from newspaper caption or supplied information.
Additional_Record_Needed: Original Montreal Gazette story, contact sheet, caption sheet, and photo reproduction.

Exact_Claim: George or Jiri Syrovatka was publicly described as Ivana's husband.
Supporting_Evidence: Library and Archives Canada item 3232626.
New_Expansion: Establishes that the husband description existed contemporaneously.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Does not prove a legal marriage; catalog spelling and provenance require review.
Additional_Record_Needed: Original Gazette caption/article; Quebec marriage search; personal papers.

Exact_Claim: Ivana's surname, marital presentation, residence chronology, and citizenship status were unusually fluid and inconsistently recorded.
Supporting_Evidence: LAC 1975 record; FBI immigration review; public chronology.
New_Expansion: Integrates Canada marital identity with FBI inconsistency concerns.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Ordinary errors, transliteration, and celebrity reporting may explain portions.
Additional_Record_Needed: Complete immigration, citizenship, university, and marriage records.

Exact_Claim: Ivana repeatedly returned to Czechoslovakia after establishing a life in the West.
Evidence_Classification: Archive reporting; contemporaneous intelligence records described
Supporting_Evidence: Radio Prague and Guardian reporting from StB files.
New_Expansion: Frames return access as a repeated operational opportunity.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Her foreign citizenship and legal-emigrant status may explain permission to enter.
Additional_Record_Needed: Passport stamps; border entries; visa and consular files.

Exact_Claim: Ivana experienced materially different consequences from prominent Czechoslovak sports defectors.
Supporting_Evidence: Navratilova, Nedomansky, and Fikotova histories; Ivana's continuing visits.
New_Expansion: The expanded chapter distinguishes legal emigration from defection while preserving the exception in effect.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Comparators had different fame, legal status, and political circumstances.
Additional_Record_Needed: Broader matched cohort of legal emigrants, foreign spouses, and athletes.

Exact_Claim: The StB began focusing on the Trump family after Ivana's marriage to Donald Trump.
Supporting_Evidence: Guardian and Radio Prague reporting from Czech Security Services Archive.
New_Expansion: Places the marriage as the opening of the documented intelligence channel.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Exact original opening order and file number still needed.
Additional_Record_Needed: StB registry entries, operative file, Zlin office index.

Exact_Claim: Milos Zelnicek provided recurring information to the StB about Ivana and Donald Trump.
Supporting_Evidence: Guardian/Respekt archive review; former StB testimony.
New_Expansion: Shows continuity from family gossip to political collection.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Cooperation appears pressured and information was sometimes benign or open-source.
Additional_Record_Needed: Complete source file, meeting reports, remuneration and tasking records.

Exact_Claim: Milos was classified as a conspiratorial informer rather than a formal recruited agent.
Supporting_Evidence: Guardian description from StB records.
New_Expansion: Preserves the precise source category and coercive context.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Translation and file terminology should be verified in original Czech.
Additional_Record_Needed: Original registration and source-category documents.

Exact_Claim: The StB ordered operational attention during Ivana's 1979 visit.
Supporting_Evidence: Guardian account of Lt. Josef Knopp memorandum.
New_Expansion: Adds date, author, visit details, and operational wording.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Original scan and full translation not yet held in repository.
Additional_Record_Needed: Knopp memorandum from Security Services Archive.

Exact_Claim: The 1979 Ivana memorandum was copied to the First Directorate section responsible for illegals.
Supporting_Evidence: Guardian review of distribution notation.
New_Expansion: Materially bolsters early foreign-intelligence routing years before Moscow trip.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Copying does not mean Ivana was an illegal agent or active operation.
Additional_Record_Needed: Original distribution sheet and receiving-section file.

Exact_Claim: An StB collaborator using the code names Langr or Chod met Ivana during return visits.
Supporting_Evidence: Radio Prague analysis of StB material.
New_Expansion: Expands the source network beyond her father.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Full identity and number of meetings remain incomplete.
Additional_Record_Needed: Collaborator file, meeting reports, code-name registry.

Exact_Claim: The StB sought information through the family channel about U.S. foreign policy, politics, economics, and intelligence agencies.
Supporting_Evidence: January 23, 1989 Surý memorandum as reported by Guardian/Respekt.
New_Expansion: Strongest evidence that the operation escalated beyond emigrant monitoring.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Original document and full Czech text not yet stored.
Additional_Record_Needed: Surý memorandum, attachments, follow-up tasking and reports.

Exact_Claim: The StB and KGB maintained formal and routine intelligence cooperation during the operation.
Supporting_Evidence: 1972 and October 1986 cooperation agreements; KGB liaison presence.
New_Expansion: Provides institutional route for sharing without assuming every document transferred.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No public document located proving a specific Trump memorandum was sent to Moscow.
Additional_Record_Needed: Liaison registers, transmission logs, KGB residentura and StB First Directorate records.

Exact_Claim: Specific StB reporting about Trump was transmitted to the KGB.
Evidence_Classification: Strong structural inference; document-specific proof missing
Supporting_Evidence: Close liaison; Soviet interest; 1987 hosting; StB foreign-intelligence routing.
New_Expansion: Reframes claim as high-probability but identifies the missing transmission record.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Former StB official could neither verify nor deny specific sharing.
Additional_Record_Needed: Transfer sheets, liaison files, Moscow archival records, Mitrokhin/Gordievsky material.

Exact_Claim: Ivana was the original human bridge linking Donald Trump to the Soviet-bloc environment before and during his first Moscow trip.
Supporting_Evidence: Marriage, family channel, StB collection, Ivana's presence on 1987 trip.
New_Expansion: Makes Chapter 1 the prehistory of Chapter 2 rather than a separate biography.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Yuri Dubinin provided the immediate Soviet invitation; Ivana was not sole pathway.
Additional_Record_Needed: Trip correspondence, visas, Intourist itinerary, Ivana papers.

Exact_Claim: The FBI opened or recommended a preliminary counterintelligence inquiry concerning Ivana.
Supporting_Evidence: FBI Vault release; February 14, 1989 memorandum reported in released files.
New_Expansion: Adds U.S. counterintelligence confirmation that the anomalies were investigated.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Underlying allegation is heavily redacted and may have been unreliable.
Additional_Record_Needed: Full declassification and appeal; case classification and serials.

Exact_Claim: The FBI inquiry tasked legal attaches in Canada and Europe to examine Ivana's emigration and associations.
Evidence_Classification: Official FBI record reported from release
Supporting_Evidence: FBI file reporting by Independent, Bloomberg, Stars and Stripes.
New_Expansion: Shows inquiry was not limited to gossip or a domestic background check.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Responses and associated names remain redacted or absent.
Additional_Record_Needed: Legat Ottawa, Montreal, Vienna, Prague and headquarters subfiles.

Exact_Claim: The FBI examined a Czech-intelligence-linked man who arranged fictitious marriages and was believed connected to Ivana.
Supporting_Evidence: Released FBI record described in multiple reports.
New_Expansion: Directly bolsters expansion of the marriage-facilitation question.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No public evidence connects him to Winklmayr or establishes wrongdoing by Ivana.
Additional_Record_Needed: Identity of subject, interview report, source reporting, referral files.

Exact_Claim: The FBI inquiry established that Ivana committed espionage or another offense.
Supporting_Evidence: No charge or public finding.
New_Expansion: The expanded work preserves the inquiry as evidence of concern, not proof of guilt.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Records are incomplete, redacted, consulted with other agencies, and partly destroyed.
Additional_Record_Needed: Closing memorandum, prosecutive decision, full release.

Exact_Claim: Ivana functioned as an intelligence-access channel regardless of whether she knowingly cooperated.
Evidence_Classification: Analytical conclusion strongly supported by documentary pattern
Supporting_Evidence: Father reporting, operational attention, collaborator contacts, political collection objectives.
New_Expansion: Clarifies that functional intelligence value does not require recruited-agent status.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Knowledge and intent remain unresolved.
Additional_Record_Needed: Ivana correspondence, StB assessments, interview records.

Exact_Claim: Ivana's emigration and marriage to Donald Trump were deliberately orchestrated as a Soviet-bloc placement operation.
Supporting_Evidence: Pattern parallels; marriage-of-convenience mechanism; later operational exploitation; FBI marriage facilitator lead.
New_Expansion: New evidence makes the hypothesis researchable rather than purely theoretical.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No direct tasking, selection, placement, or recruitment document located.
Additional_Record_Needed: Marriage facilitator identity; StB pre-1977 files; Austrian and Canadian records.
